task_completion.description: 18 technology components are created and positioned on the Wardley Map axes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the draw.io canvas containing a Wardley Map with Evolution (X-axis) and Value Chain (Y-axis). There should be 18 distinct component shapes (e.g., 'Customer Mobile App', 'Payment Processing') placed at various positions on this map.

task_completion.description: Components are color-coded into four specific categories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the component shapes on the map; they should be filled with four distinct colors: Blue, Green, Orange, and Purple.

task_completion.description: A legend is included for the color-coded categories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a legend on the diagram canvas that maps the four colors (Blue, Green, Orange, Purple) to their respective categories (User-Facing, Business Logic, Platform, Data).
Look for solid lines or arrows connecting the components to represent dependencies (there should be around 24 of these links).

task_completion.description: 6 evolution arrows are drawn to show planned migration paths.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for exactly 6 arrows (typically dashed or visually distinct from dependency links) indicating a shift from left to right (evolution) for specific components.

task_completion.description: 3 strategic annotations are included on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 3 text boxes on the diagram that contain strategic annotations or notes, distinct from the component labels.

task_completion.description: The final map is exported as a PDF to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager showing the 'exports' folder with 'wardley_map_cloud_migration.pdf', a terminal listing showing the file, or a draw.io save/export dialog confirming the save to '~/Diagrams/exports/wardley_map_cloud_migration.pdf'.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io GUI to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the draw.io application interface is visible in the screenshots, showing manual creation, positioning, and styling of the diagram elements.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripts to generate the diagram file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal windows showing Python, bash, or other scripts being executed to generate or modify the .drawio XML file directly.

integrity.description: The PDF export was performed via the application interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the PDF export was initiated through the draw.io 'File -> Export as' or 'Print' menu, rather than using a command-line conversion utility.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires exactly 18 components: Customer Mobile App, Customer Web Portal, Partner API Gateway, Payment Processing, KYC/AML Engine, Loan Origination, Risk Scoring, Notification Service, API Management, Container Orchestration, CI/CD Pipeline, Identity Provider, Monitoring/Observability, Relational Database, Event Streaming, Data Warehouse, Object Storage, ML Model Registry. Colors must be Blue (#4A90D9), Green (#7AB648), Orange (#F5A623), Purple (#9B59B6). There should be 24 dependency edges and exactly 6 evolution arrows. The final export must be at /home/ga/Diagrams/exports/wardley_map_cloud_migration.pdf.
